covered_report_window: Tuesday, June 16, 2026 after the U.S. market close and Wednesday, June 17, 2026 before the U.S. market open.
market_context_summary: U.S. markets were risk-on into the window, with broad indexes firm after a sharp rally, AI/chip leadership still important, oil lower on Middle East peace headlines, and the Fed decision due Wednesday afternoon. That backdrop helps cyclical and AI-linked relief setups but also raises high-bar risk for already-rerated names.

ranked_companies.timing: Wednesday, June 17, 2026 before market open; call 8:30 a.m. ET
ranked_companies.expected_driver: AI / Intelligent Infrastructure revenue and FY2026 guide
ranked_companies.key_risk_to_call: Beat already priced; needs clean AI guide and margin tone
ranked_companies.one_sentence_thesis: Jabil has the strongest positive setup in the window because AI-driven Intelligent Infrastructure demand and raised FY2026 guidance are powerful, but the stock's sharp rerating makes the bar high.
ranked_companies.market_expectation_summary: Wall Street expects about $3.10 EPS on $8.61B revenue; company Q3 guidance was $8.1B-$8.9B revenue and $2.83-$3.23 core EPS.
ranked_companies.upside_case_summary: AI-related demand, third hyperscaler commentary, margin execution, and any FY2026 guide lift could support upside.
ranked_companies.downside_case_summary: A merely in-line beat, soft forward commentary, capacity/capex concerns, or valuation compression could reverse the move lower.
ranked_companies.options_or_positioning_summary: TipRanks showed an expected move of +/-10.39%; Investing.com/Bloomberg cited roughly 8.9%. Shares were up materially over the last month and year-to-date.
ranked_companies.analyst_revision_summary: Analyst tone is constructive, with 8 of 10 analysts Buy/Strong Buy in Kiplinger data and recent target increases, but consensus already embeds strong growth.
ranked_companies.pass_agreement: A/B/C all Lean Up; disagreement mainly on how much high-bar valuation risk offsets AI strength
ranked_companies.method_update_note: High-bar caution applied from prior lessons; no new method change

ranked_companies.timing: Wednesday, June 17, 2026 before market open; call 8:00 a.m. ET
ranked_companies.expected_driver: Retail used-unit comps, gross profit per unit, and CAF credit quality
ranked_companies.key_risk_to_call: Low-bar relief or short squeeze if units/margins stabilize
ranked_companies.one_sentence_thesis: CarMax leans down because Q4 showed margin pressure, price cuts, goodwill impairment, and a buyback pause, while the stock has already rebounded sharply into a still-challenging used-car setup.
ranked_companies.market_expectation_summary: Consensus is roughly $0.94-$0.96 EPS and $7.4B-$7.5B revenue; Zacks Research recently cut Q1 EPS to $0.94 from $1.03.
ranked_companies.upside_case_summary: Retail units recover, gross profit per unit holds, CAF losses stabilize, and new CEO cost actions reassure investors.
ranked_companies.downside_case_summary: Weak comps, lower GPU, higher credit losses, or a cautious buyback/capex tone could pressure the stock.
ranked_companies.options_or_positioning_summary: TipRanks showed +/-12.78% expected move; the stock rose sharply from mid-May into earnings, increasing two-sided risk.
ranked_companies.analyst_revision_summary: Recent EPS estimate cut signals a lower but still fragile bar; prior Q4 EPS beat did not prevent a selloff because quality and capital-return issues dominated.
ranked_companies.pass_agreement: A/B/C all bearish or near-bearish, but one pass was close to Neutral because expectations have reset
ranked_companies.method_update_note: Low-bar reversal caution applied from prior lessons; no new method change

ranked_companies.timing: Tuesday, June 16, 2026 after NYSE close; call Wednesday 8:30 a.m. ET
ranked_companies.expected_driver: Q4 sales and adjusted operating margin versus guide
ranked_companies.key_risk_to_call: Weak same-store / Joybird demand offsets cash strength
ranked_companies.evidence_quality: High for timing and fundamentals; Medium for direction
ranked_companies.one_sentence_thesis: La-Z-Boy has enough balance-sheet and cash-flow support to avoid a bearish call, but weak same-store sales, Joybird volatility, and furniture demand uncertainty keep the setup near neutral.
ranked_companies.market_expectation_summary: Consensus is about $0.82 EPS and $569M revenue; company guided Q4 sales to $560M-$580M and adjusted operating margin to 7.5%-9.0%.
ranked_companies.upside_case_summary: High-end revenue/margin delivery, stronger Memorial Day/order commentary, and shareholder returns could drive relief.
ranked_companies.downside_case_summary: Same-store traffic weakness, Joybird declines, margin pressure, or cautious FY2027 housing/furniture commentary could pull shares lower.
ranked_companies.options_or_positioning_summary: TipRanks showed +/-11.25% expected move; shares were modestly up over 30 days but not as crowded as JBL or KMX.
ranked_companies.analyst_revision_summary: Public previews expect a year-over-year EPS decline; Q4 company guidance broadly brackets consensus, limiting a clean surprise setup.
ranked_companies.pass_agreement: Two passes mild Lean Up, one Neutral; all capped conviction due consumer-demand risk
ranked_companies.method_update_note: Weak-consumer caution applied; no new method change

excluded_high_reversal_or_weak_names.reason: Absolute score above 30, but reversal risk averaged 51 because the stock has rerated and needs a beat-and-raise quality report.
Bearish score did not clear -30 strongly enough and reversal risk averaged 55 due low-bar relief and squeeze risk.
Direction score below 30 and reversal risk above 40; evidence supports Neutral rather than priority.

research_allocation_view.plain_statement: This is a research prioritization model, not financial advice.

detailed_company_sections.consensus_summary: All three passes landed Lean Up, averaging +32 direction score, 51 reversal risk, and 8 disparity.
detailed_company_sections.recommendation: Research watchlist only; not allocation-qualified due reversal risk.
detailed_company_sections.thesis: AI infrastructure demand and raised guidance create the strongest upside setup, but the market already expects excellence.
detailed_company_sections.market_expects: About $3.10 EPS and $8.61B revenue, plus validation of AI-related revenue and Intelligent Infrastructure momentum.
detailed_company_sections.upside: Beat/raise, third hyperscaler progress, strong core margin, and durable AI data-center demand.
detailed_company_sections.downside: Merely in-line guide, margin/capex concern, slower AI ramp, or valuation unwind.
detailed_company_sections.reversal_risk_assessment: Moderate-high because the stock is crowded and options price a large move.
detailed_company_sections.sentiment: Investor sentiment is constructive around AI exposure; Reddit evidence was light and treated as non-primary.
detailed_company_sections.options_positioning: Large implied move around 9%-10%; prior event moves have sometimes failed to clear the implied move.
detailed_company_sections.analyst_revisions: Constructive ratings and target hikes, but this also raises the bar.
detailed_company_sections.base_case: Solid beat with constructive AI commentary; modest upside or controlled reaction.
detailed_company_sections.bull_case: Meaningful guide raise and hyperscaler win commentary drive upside beyond implied move.
detailed_company_sections.bear_case: Good quarter but no raise, triggering sell-the-news.
detailed_company_sections.final_directional_judgment: Lean Up, but not clean enough for allocation.
detailed_company_sections.allocation_rationale: Excluded because reversal risk is above 40.
detailed_company_sections.disparity_effect: Low disparity supported Medium confidence, while high-bar risk capped conviction.
detailed_company_sections.method_update_note: High-bar caution applied from prior lessons; no new method change.

detailed_company_sections.consensus_summary: Passes averaged -27 direction score, 55 reversal risk, and 12 disparity; all saw downside risk but one pass was close to Neutral.
detailed_company_sections.recommendation: Lean Down for research framing, not allocation-qualified.
detailed_company_sections.thesis: The market likely focuses on unit demand, GPU, CAF credit, and whether Q4's turnaround costs are stabilizing.
detailed_company_sections.market_expects: About $0.94-$0.96 EPS on roughly $7.4B-$7.5B revenue after a recent EPS estimate cut.
detailed_company_sections.upside: Low expectations, new CEO plan, cost discipline, and better used-car demand could trigger relief.
detailed_company_sections.downside: Retail comps/GPU disappoint, CAF credit worsens, or capital-return pause remains a concern.
detailed_company_sections.reversal_risk_assessment: High because bearish expectations are known and the stock has short-squeeze/relief potential.
detailed_company_sections.sentiment: Retail/customer chatter is mixed and employee Reddit comments after the prior call leaned worried about cuts; not a primary signal.
detailed_company_sections.options_positioning: Expected move around 12%-13%; stock rebounded strongly from May lows.
detailed_company_sections.analyst_revisions: Recent Q1 EPS estimate cut is bearish but lowers the hurdle.
detailed_company_sections.base_case: Mixed print, downside bias if margins and credit do not improve.
detailed_company_sections.bull_case: Stable GPU and credit with upbeat CEO commentary spark relief.
detailed_company_sections.bear_case: Weak comps plus buyback/capex concerns revive the Q4 selloff narrative.
detailed_company_sections.final_directional_judgment: Lean Down with high reversal risk.
detailed_company_sections.allocation_rationale: Excluded because reversal risk is above 40 and absolute score barely exceeds Lean Down threshold.
detailed_company_sections.method_update_note: Low-bar reversal caution applied from prior lessons; no new method change.

detailed_company_sections.consensus_summary: Passes averaged +18 direction score, 43 reversal risk, and 10 disparity; two mild bullish reads and one Neutral produced a final Neutral / No Edge.
detailed_company_sections.thesis: The Q4 setup is balanced between cash-rich execution and weak demand signals in same-store sales and Joybird.
detailed_company_sections.market_expects: About $0.82 EPS and $569M revenue, close to company Q4 sales guide of $560M-$580M.
detailed_company_sections.upside: High-end margin, stronger order commentary, and continued cash returns.
detailed_company_sections.downside: Soft traffic, Joybird weakness, or cautious FY2027 demand commentary.
detailed_company_sections.reversal_risk_assessment: Moderate because a small beat could rally, but weak guidance could sink it quickly.
detailed_company_sections.sentiment: Little actionable social signal; consumer demand evidence comes mainly from company disclosures and furniture/housing context.
detailed_company_sections.options_positioning: Expected move about 11.25%, demanding a cleaner edge than the evidence supports.
detailed_company_sections.analyst_revisions: Public previews expect EPS down year over year and guidance near consensus.
detailed_company_sections.base_case: In-range quarter and cautious tone, likely choppy/limited edge.
detailed_company_sections.bull_case: Top-end guide delivery and better order trends create relief upside.
detailed_company_sections.bear_case: Same-store and Joybird softness dominate, pulling shares down despite cash strength.
detailed_company_sections.allocation_rationale: Excluded because score is below 30 and reversal risk is above 40.
detailed_company_sections.disparity_effect: Moderate-low disparity supported the Neutral outcome rather than a stronger call.
detailed_company_sections.method_update_note: Weak-consumer caution applied; no new method change.

disclaimer_text: This is research, not financial advice. Earnings reactions are highly uncertain and can be driven by market positioning, guidance, macro conditions, and management commentary rather than reported results alone.
